mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market demand and future consumption capacity. This drives positive equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 158,638 thousand persons indicates sustained employment above trend
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Payroll growth remains positive but S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 100,000 jobs, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ months, the cost of capital rises sharply, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of December 2009, indicating tight monetary conditions
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated real rates
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 5-day forward returns is positive (r > 0.1) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening aggregate demand and future earnings headwinds. This dual deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 3% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,277 thousand units and RSAFS at 703,088 million dollars as of November 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite joint HOUST and RSAFS declines, or the interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 103.0 index points indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. This supports positive corporate margins and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.1168 as of November 2009, indicating post-recession recovery in manufacturing
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 103.0 is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% and increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger equity risk-off behavior, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of December 2009, indicating labor market stabilization post-crisis
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase with rising joblessness
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE spike, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, real rates rise sharply, compressing equity valuations. This joint condition produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth and discount rates simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 313.049 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of December 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite rising real rates, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly above 2.5% annualized signals sustained economic expansion and earnings growth. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward in expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22,758.752 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q3 2009, indicating post-recession recovery
regime_hint: economic expansion with positive growth momentum
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 1% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) remain below 1,300 thousand units, it signals weakening consumer demand and housing market fragility. This dual weakness produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 1.0% AND HOUST < 1,300 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 703,088 million dollars and HOUST at 1,277 thousand units as of November 2009
regime_hint: demand-constrained recovery with housing market weakness
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite weak retail and housing, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
